flag: Keep (adopt) undo archives then session-requeues; inbox file not restored on disk
closestEquivalent: Undo archives the registry entry and re-queues a session-only seedling overlay so shell/rot/triage counts rise and the row is actionable again. Re-Keep on a session seedling unarchives via POST /api/registry/move (not adopt). Timed Discard on a session seedling drops the overlay without DELETE. Reload loses the session overlay (no reverse-adopt API / no inbox file recreation).

flag: Discard uses client soft-delete until undo window lapses
closestEquivalent: Discard immediately hides the seedling in the queue and shows the undo ribbon. DELETE /api/inbox?path=... runs only when the ~8s ribbon times out, is dismissed, or is replaced by a later discard. Undo within the window re-queues without server delete. Session-only requeues skip DELETE.

No server endpoint consolidates duplicate skills; Merge dismisses the inbox row in-session (optional AI dedupe hint / target picker). Undo re-queues within the session only; inbox file remains on disk until a later real discard commits.
Tiers remain localStorage-backed; Undo restores prior tier client-side.

flag: Deploy commit has no full reverse sync
closestEquivalent: Post-commit messaging is honest: deployed counts only; no fake undo ribbon for full harness restore.
SyncReport has created/pruned/fixed (no drift field). Drift section lists dry-run fixed as corrections and supplements with status.drift names when present.

flag: Bulk Keep all suggested has no undo ribbon with session re-queue
closestEquivalent: Bulk adopt posts many items at once; ribbon undoes via multi-archive using names only (batchPaths holds names, no seedling snapshots), so session re-queue of each source path is incomplete. Individual Keep still has archive + session re-queue undo. Bulk keep is secondary.
